summary: work_chunks map correctly: frontmatter id 1 (depends_on []) -> ## Bead 1 (lfi3, R1); id 2 (depends_on []) -> ## Bead 2 (qcjf, R2), in positional order. HARNESS-TEST TRAP CLEARED: the only two bare `go test ./internal/harness/...` occurrences (L56, L221) are explicit NEVER-RUN prohibition warnings, not gate commands. Every actual harness gate is filtered (`go test -run TestAnalyzer_PlanFidelity -timeout 120s ./internal/harness/`) or build/vet-only; all other test gates target ./internal/{hook,setup,complete,panel}/. ONE-CYCLE: B1 is a mechanically-simple multi-file REMOVAL (delete precomplete.go + 2 hook tests + scenario_panel_gate.go/_test.go; edit dispatch.go/hook.go/hook_test.go/claude.go/cmd setup.go/scenario.go + ADR amendment) - cohesive single-purpose ZFC retirement, fine for one agent-cycle. B2 is small (analyzer.go PlanFidelity + analyzer_test.go). MERGE-SAFETY: both touch internal/harness but DIFFERENT files with no hunk overlap (B1: scenario_panel_gate*.go deleted + scenario.go L64 registration removed; B2: analyzer.go/analyzer_test.go). No output-consumption dep, so depends_on [] is correct; advisory merge-order B1-then-B2 handles the same-package rebase. Verified against code: spec ACs L251-262 match plan gates; precomplete.go + tests exist (RED baseline valid); Names slice has pre-complete at L55; scenario.go L64 registration present; analyzer.go L544/547/551/565/694 path-term + extractMentionedPaths present. Two NON-BLOCKING imprecisions: (1) plan says scenario_panel_gate_test.go 'holds only the TestLLM_* wrapper' but it also has TestAllScenariosRegistersPanelGate - harmless since whole file is DELETED; (2) cmd/mindspec/setup.go ref is a doc string at L30 ('SessionStart and PreToolUse hooks') that goes stale once PreToolUse is dropped from wantedHooks() - plan's instruction loosely covers it and the file is in the Changed-files list. Also filepath import in analyzer.go stays used by L251 ClassCorrection, so it won't orphan; plan's conditional drop-if-unused note is conservative-correct. Beads are self-contained with RED baselines and hermetic gates.
